entries.cleaned_md: You've arrived too late! The orcs of the [Deathfist Empire](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Deathfist_Orcs_(Faction)) have already beaten Kaljanar and take her ring of teleportation to [Deathfist Citadel](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Deathfist_Citadel). Make haste!

## Access

- On normal servers, there is no longer an access quest. However, on TLE servers, at least one member of the raid must have completed the access questline.
- The flame barrier has been removed from Deathtoll. However, each member of the raid will need to complete [A Mark of Awakening](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_Mark_of_Awakening) and equip the reward to pass through the location where the flame barrier was (where the basilisk is standing). After you've crossed that point, you can replace it with your regular necklace. You do not need the Mark of Awakening equipped for boss fights, it only needs to be in your character's inventory.

## Trash

When you first zone in, there are droags immediately below the ledge you're standing on, as well as two gazers and a flying serpent which move around the area. The gazers can be lethal if they are not pulled separately and turned away from the raid as quickly as possible. Be careful when range-pulling them, they are social.

There are traps throughout the zone which spawn [carrion worms](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_carrion_worm). It is recommended the raid avoid central areas on the ground and stay as close to the walls as possible. Everyone should follow the same path through the zone and should not move ahead of the tanks. Although Deathtoll is a persistent raid zone, if you leave and return later, the carrion worm traps will reset and can be triggered again.

There are invisible gazers in the area near Fitzpitzle and the hallways leading to the Amorphous Drake. The gazers frequently cast high-damage AoE DoTs (Eye of Fire or Eye of Frost) which must be cured immediately.

## Progression

Before you engage [Tarinax the Destroyer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tarinax_the_Destroyer), you must first defeat [Yitzik the Hurler](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Yitzik_the_Hurler), [Fitzpitzle](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fitzpitzle_(Deathtoll)) and [Cruor Alluvium](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Cruor_Alluvium).

> If any of these bosses are still alive when you engage Tarinax, he will immediately kill everyone in the raid.

You are not required to kill the Amorphous Drake.

Sanctum chaperones wander throughout the instance and respawn. They will aggro even if greyed out. Once in their proximity they will run to nearby stoneskin destroyers and activate them. They also have an auto-stun/silence on the first player they hit. The best way to deal with them is to attack them from afar so they won't aggro the gargoyles.

## Progression

There is no hard and fast rule for this zone, giving the raid a bit of choice in kill order.  The first two named mobs (Demetrius Crane and Dreadlord D'Somni) are considered to be comparatively straightforward kills and therefore good practice for guilds beginning to raid.  However, the difficulty level rises sharply after that point.

Different raids have their own preference on which one of these two named mobs to kill first.  However, the stategy for each involves clearing the trash mobs near the zone in and all trash leading up to and surrounding them.

## Trash

This stage is fairly straightforward as long as you remain aware of three things:

- The trash mobs wander a fairly wide area so it is important to choose a fighting position where the raid can avoid adds; the [spite golems](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_spite_golem) move quite quickly, so be aware of their wandering radius.
- In some areas, there are pairs of [animus haunted chests](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/an_animus_haunted_chest) very close to each other, making a potentially tricky pull.  Do not precast anything (heals, reactives etc) before the tank pulls or you will get both chests heading straight towards the culprit!
- Look above to make sure the flying mobs ([scorn fiends](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_scorn_fiend)) do not add.

entries.zone_name: The Eternal Gorge: A Daring Confrontation
entries.cleaned_md:
## Prep & Initial Trash

Get ready at the top at zone in (buffs, etc). Everyone go downstairs but stay inside tower, have the tank step outside and pull mobs in until the area around the tower is clear. Avoid using out-of-encounter AEs (blue background, etc) because they can hit mobs wandering around the tower and add.

## Progression

Once the tower area is clear, start pulling each mausoleum. There is a named in each with a couple guards. These named will memwipe and aggro a certain class frequently, calling out the name of the person they are going to attack. See map for the type of class each sister will aggro on memwiping. Healers include power healing, so enchanters should watch out on two mobs.

Once all 4 sisters are killed, the "shield" around the Mother is gone and she can be attacked.

## Notes

> The zone does not lock until you kill the Mother — if she bugs out and becomes non-attackable after repeated wipes, you can reset.

## Progression

Upon entering the zone, all the named skeleton mobs are non-aggro and unattackable. To begin the storyline, search carefully for [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) in the room with the unactivated Doomcoil and select the option to make a deal with her via right click. She will tell you a tale about her "raid force" and discuss the possibility of a group effort between your raid and her force. After speaking to Ludmila, have a priest (a dirge also works) resurrect [Jracol Binari](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jracol_Binari) (again via right click and not the actual spell).

Once Jracol Binari is resurrected, have a member of the raid take the [Ancient Sebilisian Shards](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ancient_Sebilisian_Shards) from [Meldrath Kloktik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Meldrath_Kloktik) via right-click. These shards (which are a single item) are used to activate the Vyx skeletons ([Adkar Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Adkar_Vyx), [Iztapa Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Iztapa_Vyx), [Wymbulu Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Wymbulu_Vyx) and [Zykluk Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Zykluk_Vyx)). Proceed around the rooms of the zone to kill these four skeletons. The order of their deaths doesn't matter for the storyline, but each is slightly different from the others and a successful kill therefore requires a specific resist setup and strategy (see the appropriate named information page for more details).

Each time one of these skeletons is killed, a random member of the raid will receive a shard in their inventory. These four (tradeable) shards are needed to spawn the next named ([Doomcoil](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Doomcoil)). Once all four skeletons are killed and the shards obtained, trade them all to one person and return to the room with Ludmilla and her raid force.

Move the whole raid into Doomcoil's room and have the person with the 4 shards right click and give the shards to the Meldrath. The adventurers will slowly leave and then after a short pause to talk, they will spawn Doomcoil and cause barrier wards to go up around the room preventing your raids exit. You get one shot at beating Doomcoil before getting locked out of the room.

> Unfortunately, if left alive, Doomcoil can add in later fights.

Kill the NPC raid force (an encounter based around [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov)) and then [Imzok's Revenge](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Imzok's_Revenge). If you fail to kill Doomcoil, killing Meldrath first in the adventurers encounter will prevent him from being summoned.

## Access

You must have killed [Tairiza the Widow Mistress](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tairiza_the_Widow_Mistress) and collected an item ( Mistmyrian Soulcube ) from her if you want to have any chance at killing VS. Give the item from Tairiza to someone that isn't urgently needed when fighting VS.

## Trash

Zone in to the main room. The hall directly in front of you will spawn 2 84x4 mobs once you cross the threshold between the main room and the hall.
